channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Study Suggests Ancient Greenland Cooling Event Weakened Indian Monsoon
item.description: A recent study highlights that approximately 8,200 years ago, a significant cooling event occurred in Greenland, which had far-reaching impacts on global climate patterns. This research indicates that...

item.title: Nvidia Launches NemoClaw to Provide Enterprise-Grade Security for AI Agents
item.description: During the 2026 GTC keynote, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang introduced NemoClaw, a new enterprise AI agent platform built on top of the open-source OpenClaw framework. NemoClaw enhances OpenClaw with advance...

item.title: Canadian Filmmakers Achieve Multiple Wins at the 2026 Academy Awards
item.description: Montreal-based filmmakers Chris Lavis and Maciek Szczerbowski have brought Canada into the spotlight at the 2026 Academy Awards by winning the Oscar for Best Animated Short Film with their stop-motion...

item.title: Tanzania, Mozambique and Malawi Unite to Protect Ruvuma River Basin with GEF Support
item.description: Tanzania, Mozambique na Malawi wameshirikiana kuanzisha mradi wa dola 7.12 million kupitia Global Environment Facility (GEF) kulinda mto Ruvuma Basin. Mto huu mkubwa unaunganisha milima ya kusini mwa ...

item.title: British Columbia recruits over 400 U.S. health-care professionals to address staffing needs
item.description: Between March 2025 and January 2026, British Columbia successfully recruited 414 health-care workers from the United States, including 89 doctors, 42 nurse practitioners, 260 nurses, and 23 allied hea...

item.title: Former DPWH Official Testifies About P130-M Kickbacks from Zaldy Co
item.description: Former DPWH Undersecretary Roberto Bernardo testified before the Sandiganbayan Sixth Division that former Ako Bicol party-list Rep. Zaldy Co gave him a total of P130 million as a 'balato,' or token of...

item.title: Trump criticises UK stance on Strait of Hormuz amid Iran tensions
item.description: US President Donald Trump has expressed disappointment with the United Kingdom over its response to the escalating Iran conflict, particularly regarding efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a criti...

item.title: Bunnie Xo shares honest look at recovery from recent facelift
item.description: Bunnie Xo, wife of country artist Jelly Roll and host of the 'Dumb Blonde' podcast, has shared an unfiltered look at her facelift recovery. The 46-year-old posted a candid video on Instagram showing h...

item.title: Andrew Ollett on Prakrit as a Literary Language and Its Cultural Significance
item.description: Professor Andrew Ollett, a scholar at the University of Chicago, has been awarded the Infosys Prize 2025 in Humanities and Social Sciences for his work on the Prakrit language. His book, 'Languages of...

item.title: Revive Australia: Key Figures and Political Background Explained
item.description: Revive Australia is a newly formed right-wing political group generating curiosity about its leadership and funding. The organisation is led by Nathan Porter, a former committed Liberal now aligned wi...

item.title: The lasting mental impact of cyclones on Beira’s residents
item.description: Six years after Cyclone Idai devastated Beira, the city remains haunted by both physical and psychological scars. Residents live in a state of constant vigilance as new storms, like Cyclone Gezani, fo...

item.title: Health CS Clarifies Nairobi Hospital Ownership Amid Governance Crisis
item.description: Health Cabinet Secretary Aden Duale has cleared the air on rumours claiming the government is moving to take over The Nairobi Hospital. Speaking to Capital News, Duale explained that the hospital is s...

item.title: Rising Gas, Food, and Travel Costs in B.C. Linked to Middle East Conflict
item.description: The ongoing conflict in Iran is affecting global oil supplies and driving up energy prices, with noticeable impacts in British Columbia. Gas prices in Metro Vancouver have reached record highs above $...

item.title: Opposition Calls for Legal Action over Tuju’s Property Dispute
item.description: In Murang'a, leaders of the United Alternative Government have raised strong objections to what they describe as an illegal takeover of property belonging to former Cabinet minister Raphael Tuju. Spea...

item.title: PTI Seeks Disqualification of Senator Saifullah Abro Over Voting Against Party Directive
item.description: ISLAMABAD: The Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf (PTI) chairman, Barrister Gohar Ali Khan, has formally submitted a reference to the Senate secretariat seeking the disqualification of Senator Saifullah Abro. T...

item.title: Elon Musk’s Doge Initiative: Attempting to Transform Government Through Technology and Gaming
item.description: In 2025, Elon Musk assumed a de facto leadership role in the US government through a department focused on efficiency, bringing a mindset heavily influenced by gaming, startup culture, and right-wing ...

item.title: Minor fire reported at Thiruvananthapuram Medical College hospital, patients safely evacuated
item.description: On the morning of March 17, 2026, a minor fire was reported at the surgical trauma care unit of the Government Medical College Hospital in Thiruvananthapuram at around 9:45 a.m. Fortunately, no casual...

item.title: Cross River Governor Orders Review of Sanitation Operations in Calabar Metropolis
item.description: Cross River State Governor, Bassey Otu, don announce say e wan make dem immediately review how dem dey run sanitation operations for Calabar metropolis. According to im Chief Press Secretary, Linus Ob...

item.title: MILF suspends engagement with government until new peace panel chair is appointed
item.description: The Moro Islamic Liberation Front (MILF) announced a temporary suspension of key negotiations with the Philippine national government, citing the absence of a full-fledged chairman for the Government ...

item.title: Why normalising the loss of human life in wars must stop
item.description: The article by Shimbo Pastory reflects on how modern society and the global media have grown disturbingly comfortable with wars that disregard human life. It argues that many people now treat wars lik...
